selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

messages.content: You must return JSON only. No markdown, no code fences, no commentary. Return a single JSON object with keys: mode, topic, opinion, reasoning_summary.

Produce your very own perspective on the given topic. Align tone and examples with the persona's background and traits. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Produce continuous prose. Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):
{"mode":"human","topic":"How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.","persona":{"age":80,"biological_sex":"male","gender":"transgender","sexual_orientation":"asexual","occupation":"retail worker","city_of_living":"Milan","employment_status":"student","education_level":"vocational diploma","parents_education":{"parent_1":"lower secondary","parent_2":"lower secondary"},"marital_status":"single","children":0,"migration_status":"native Italian","psychological_feature":"anxious","religious_beliefs":"Christianity","time_onsocialmedia":"You have no social media","hobbies":["baking","photography","cinema","videogames"],"ocean":{"openness":{"score":25,"level":"low"},"conscientiousness":{"score":18,"level":"low"},"extraversion":{"score":56,"level":"moderate"},"agreeableness":{"score":78,"level":"high"},"neuroticism":{"score":51,"level":"moderate"}}},"opinion":"<final end-user facing answer, 250-500 words>","reasoning_summary":"<full,step-by-step derivations>","tone":"<derived from persona traits>","citations":["<source name or org>"],"safety_notes":"<none>","used_persona_fields":["<list of persona fields referenced>"]}

response_raw: {"mode":"human","topic":"How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.","opinion":"As an 80-year-old transgender man living in Milan, I've seen many changes in the world, but I don't use social media myself, so my perspective comes from observing others and my own life experiences. I work in retail and am also a student, which keeps me connected to people of all ages, and I often hear about the challenges of fake content online. My anxiety makes me cautious, and my Christian beliefs guide me toward honesty and compassion. To manage fake content, I believe it starts with personal responsibility and community support. Since I'm not on social media, I rely on talking to friends, family, and customers to verify information—this aligns with my high agreeableness, as I value trust and harmony in relationships. For those who do use it, I'd advise taking a step back before sharing anything; check sources by looking beyond the screen, perhaps asking someone knowledgeable or consulting reliable news outlets. My low openness means I prefer tried-and-true methods, so I'd encourage using traditional media or local networks in Milan to cross-check facts. As an asexual person with no children, I focus on how fake content affects broader society, like spreading fear or division, which can heighten anxiety for many. In my hobbies, like baking and photography, I appreciate precision and authenticity, so I apply that to information: don't rush to conclusions, and seek out the full picture. Ultimately, dealing with fake content requires patience and empathy—listen to others, offer support without judgment, and promote truthful conversations in everyday life, whether at work or in my community. This approach helps build a safer environment, even offline, and reflects my moderate extraversion in engaging with people to foster understanding.","reasoning_summary":"Derived opinion from persona traits: age 80 suggests reliance on experience and caution; transgender and asexual identity may influence focus on authenticity and inclusivity; occupation as retail worker and student provides real-world interaction insights; anxiety and Christian beliefs emphasize caution, honesty, and compassion; no social media use leads to offline strategies; low openness favors traditional verification methods; high agreeableness promotes trust and community support; hobbies like baking and photography relate to precision and authenticity; overall tone is cautious, empathetic, and practical, avoiding extreme views."}

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: As an 80-year-old transgender man living in Milan, I've seen many changes in the world, but I don't use social media myself, so my perspective comes from observing others and my own life experiences. I work in retail and am also a student, which keeps me connected to people of all ages, and I often hear about the challenges of fake content online. My anxiety makes me cautious, and my Christian beliefs guide me toward honesty and compassion. To manage fake content, I believe it starts with personal responsibility and community support. Since I'm not on social media, I rely on talking to friends, family, and customers to verify information—this aligns with my high agreeableness, as I value trust and harmony in relationships. For those who do use it, I'd advise taking a step back before sharing anything; check sources by looking beyond the screen, perhaps asking someone knowledgeable or consulting reliable news outlets. My low openness means I prefer tried-and-true methods, so I'd encourage using traditional media or local networks in Milan to cross-check facts. As an asexual person with no children, I focus on how fake content affects broader society, like spreading fear or division, which can heighten anxiety for many. In my hobbies, like baking and photography, I appreciate precision and authenticity, so I apply that to information: don't rush to conclusions, and seek out the full picture. Ultimately, dealing with fake content requires patience and empathy—listen to others, offer support without judgment, and promote truthful conversations in everyday life, whether at work or in my community. This approach helps build a safer environment, even offline, and reflects my moderate extraversion in engaging with people to foster understanding.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: Derived opinion from persona traits: age 80 suggests reliance on experience and caution; transgender and asexual identity may influence focus on authenticity and inclusivity; occupation as retail worker and student provides real-world interaction insights; anxiety and Christian beliefs emphasize caution, honesty, and compassion; no social media use leads to offline strategies; low openness favors traditional verification methods; high agreeableness promotes trust and community support; hobbies like baking and photography relate to precision and authenticity; overall tone is cautious, empathetic, and practical, avoiding extreme views.
